trans-unit.source: Sketch Map Tool Exercise 3 - Role Play using the Sketch Map Tool in EVCA
trans-unit.target: Sketch Map Tool Exercise 3 - Role Play using the Sketch Map Tool in EVCA

trans-unit.source: Characteristics of the exercise
trans-unit.target: Characteristics of the exercise

trans-unit.source: :::{card} __Aim of this exercise:__ ^^^ Explore Sketch Mapping in a roleplay, either in the perspective of a mapping facilitator, or as a participant and learn more about the challenges during the mapping. :::
trans-unit.target: :::{card} __Aim of this exercise:__ ^^^ Explore Sketch Mapping in a roleplay, either in the perspective of a mapping facilitator, or as a participant and learn more about the challenges during the mapping. :::

trans-unit.source: :::{grid-item-card} __Type of trainings exercise:__ ^^^ This exercise can only be conducted in __presence training__ and is based on na role-play and discussions.
trans-unit.target: :::{grid-item-card} __Type of trainings exercise:__ ^^^ This exercise can only be conducted in __presence training__ and is based on na role-play and discussions.

trans-unit.source: Planners/facilitators, as well as practitioners who are involved in the preparation of the field campaign or collecting the data themselves.
trans-unit.target: Planners/facilitators, as well as practitioners who are involved in the preparation of the field campaign or collecting the data themselves.

trans-unit.source: Beginners-level: no specific knowledge about QGIS/uMap required
trans-unit.target: Beginners-level: no specific knowledge about QGIS/uMap required

trans-unit.source: Phase of participatory/community mapping
trans-unit.target: Phase of participatory/community mapping

trans-unit.source: Participatory mapping
trans-unit.target: Participatory mapping

trans-unit.source: Available data and resources
trans-unit.target: Available data and resources

trans-unit.source: [Introductory slides on the Sketch Map Tool](https://nexus.heigit.org/repository/gis-training-resource-center/mobile_data_collection/sketch_map_tool/PPP/Introduction_Sketch_Map_Tool_21_03.pptx)
trans-unit.target: [Introductory slides on the Sketch Map Tool](https://nexus.heigit.org/repository/gis-training-resource-center/mobile_data_collection/sketch_map_tool/PPP/Introduction_Sketch_Map_Tool_21_03.pptx)

trans-unit.source: Case studies descriptions:
trans-unit.target: Case studies descriptions:

trans-unit.source: [Case Study 1](https://nexus.heigit.org/repository/gis-training-resource-center/mobile_data_collection/sketch_map_tool_training/Case_Study_1.pdf)
trans-unit.target: [Case Study 1](https://nexus.heigit.org/repository/gis-training-resource-center/mobile_data_collection/sketch_map_tool_training/Case_Study_1.pdf)

trans-unit.source: [Case Study 2](https://nexus.heigit.org/repository/gis-training-resource-center/mobile_data_collection/sketch_map_tool_training/Case_Study_2.pdf)
trans-unit.target: [Case Study 2](https://nexus.heigit.org/repository/gis-training-resource-center/mobile_data_collection/sketch_map_tool_training/Case_Study_2.pdf)

trans-unit.source: [Case Study 3](https://nexus.heigit.org/repository/gis-training-resource-center/mobile_data_collection/sketch_map_tool_training/Case_Study_3.pdf)
trans-unit.target: [Case Study 3](https://nexus.heigit.org/repository/gis-training-resource-center/mobile_data_collection/sketch_map_tool_training/Case_Study_3.pdf)

trans-unit.source: Case studies:
trans-unit.target: Case studies:

trans-unit.source: [Case Study Map 1](https://nexus.heigit.org/repository/gis-training-resource-center/mobile_data_collection/sketch_map_tool_training/Sketch_Map_Tool_Exercise_2_and_3/Case_1_Sudan.zip),
trans-unit.target: [Case Study Map 1](https://nexus.heigit.org/repository/gis-training-resource-center/mobile_data_collection/sketch_map_tool_training/Sketch_Map_Tool_Exercise_2_and_3/Case_1_Sudan.zip),

trans-unit.source: [Case Study Map 2](https://nexus.heigit.org/repository/gis-training-resource-center/mobile_data_collection/sketch_map_tool_training/Sketch_Map_Tool_Exercise_2_and_3/Case_2_Bolivien.zip),
trans-unit.target: [Case Study Map 2](https://nexus.heigit.org/repository/gis-training-resource-center/mobile_data_collection/sketch_map_tool_training/Sketch_Map_Tool_Exercise_2_and_3/Case_2_Bolivien.zip),

trans-unit.source: [Case Study Map 3](https://nexus.heigit.org/repository/gis-training-resource-center/mobile_data_collection/sketch_map_tool_training/Sketch_Map_Tool_Exercise_2_and_3/Case_3_Port_Harcourt.zip),
trans-unit.target: [Case Study Map 3](https://nexus.heigit.org/repository/gis-training-resource-center/mobile_data_collection/sketch_map_tool_training/Sketch_Map_Tool_Exercise_2_and_3/Case_3_Port_Harcourt.zip),

trans-unit.source: [Printable exercise factsheet](https://nexus.heigit.org/repository/gis-training-resource-center/mobile_data_collection/sketch_map_tool_training/Factsheet_printing_%20Ex_3.pdf)
trans-unit.target: [Printable exercise factsheet](https://nexus.heigit.org/repository/gis-training-resource-center/mobile_data_collection/sketch_map_tool_training/Factsheet_printing_%20Ex_3.pdf)

trans-unit.source: Instructions for the trainers
trans-unit.target: Instructions for the trainers

trans-unit.source: :::{dropdown} __Trainers Corner__
trans-unit.target: :::{dropdown} __Trainers Corner__

trans-unit.source: Prepare the training
trans-unit.target: Prepare the training

trans-unit.source: Take the time to familiarise yourself with the provided material for the exercise and the Sketch Map Tool in general.
trans-unit.target: Take the time to familiarise yourself with the provided material for the exercise and the Sketch Map Tool in general.

trans-unit.source: Check out [How to do trainings?](/content/Trainers_corner/en_how_to_training.md) for some general tips on training conduction
trans-unit.target: Check out [How to do trainings?](/content/Trainers_corner/en_how_to_training.md) for some general tips on training conduction

trans-unit.source: Conduct the training:
trans-unit.target: Conduct the training:

trans-unit.source: __Introduction:__
trans-unit.target: __Introduction:__

trans-unit.source: Introduce the idea, the aim and the benefits of a Sketch Map. If you are focusing on EVCA you might use the [EVCA Sketch Map Tool Slides]()
trans-unit.target: Introduce the idea, the aim and the benefits of a Sketch Map. If you are focusing on EVCA you might use the [EVCA Sketch Map Tool Slides]()

trans-unit.source: Provide access to the needed material.
trans-unit.target: Provide access to the needed material.

trans-unit.source: Organize the groups (e.g. create breakout-rooms) and assign the groups with their case. The group sizes shouldn't be too big, so everyone can contribute. For each case assign 2-3 participants the role of facilitators and 4-7 participants the role of community members.
trans-unit.target: Organize the groups (e.g. create breakout-rooms) and assign the groups with their case. The group sizes shouldn't be too big, so everyone can contribute. For each case assign 2-3 participants the role of facilitators and 4-7 participants the role of community members.

trans-unit.source: __Time for group work:__
trans-unit.target: __Time for group work:__

trans-unit.source: Check-in with the groups if there are questions or problems.
trans-unit.target: Check-in with the groups if there are questions or problems.

trans-unit.source: Prepare the presentation tool for the Wrap-Up.
trans-unit.target: Prepare the presentation tool for the Wrap-Up.

trans-unit.source: __Wrap up:__
trans-unit.target: __Wrap up:__

trans-unit.source: Take some time at the end to wrap up where the group will present their findings.
trans-unit.target: Take some time at the end to wrap up where the group will present their findings.

trans-unit.source: Summarize challenges of the participatory mapping approach.
trans-unit.target: Summarize challenges of the participatory mapping approach.

trans-unit.source: Discuss benefits of showing results as a map
trans-unit.target: Discuss benefits of showing results as a map

trans-unit.source: Leave time for open questions. ::::
trans-unit.target: Leave time for open questions. ::::

trans-unit.source: Background Information
trans-unit.target: Background Information

trans-unit.source: Enhanced Vulnerability and Capacity Assessment (EVCA)
trans-unit.target: Enhanced Vulnerability and Capacity Assessment (EVCA)

trans-unit.source: In order to spatially assess and identify risk factors, the EVCA recommends to conduct a mapping activity throughout the process where community members jointly create a spatial map, hazard & exposure map and vulnerability & capacity map (see [EVCA Toolbox](https://preparecenter.org/site/evca/toolbox/)). Mapping is done with paper and pen and generally on blank paper. The Sketch Map Tool has great potential to support a sustainable participatory mapping process by digitising the process while, at the same time, keeping it simple.
trans-unit.target: In order to spatially assess and identify risk factors, the EVCA recommends to conduct a mapping activity throughout the process where community members jointly create a spatial map, hazard & exposure map and vulnerability & capacity map (see [EVCA Toolbox](https://preparecenter.org/site/evca/toolbox/)). Mapping is done with paper and pen and generally on blank paper. The Sketch Map Tool has great potential to support a sustainable participatory mapping process by digitising the process while, at the same time, keeping it simple.

trans-unit.source: Benefits of the Sketch Map Tool
trans-unit.target: Benefits of the Sketch Map Tool

trans-unit.source: __During the data collection:__
trans-unit.target: __During the data collection:__

trans-unit.source: Maps provide orientation and can guide discussions.
trans-unit.target: Maps provide orientation and can guide discussions.

trans-unit.source: With the sketching you achieve an immediate visualization of local knowledge.
trans-unit.target: With the sketching you achieve an immediate visualization of local knowledge.

trans-unit.source: __After the data collection:__
trans-unit.target: __After the data collection:__

trans-unit.source: Displaying and sharing the result.
trans-unit.target: Displaying and sharing the result.

trans-unit.source: Integrate and analyse local knowledge in GIS-software such as QGIS
trans-unit.target: Integrate and analyse local knowledge in GIS-software such as QGIS

trans-unit.source: Step-by-step introduction for participants
trans-unit.target: Step-by-step introduction for participants

trans-unit.source: Exercise: Roleplay
trans-unit.target: Exercise: Roleplay

trans-unit.source: Participatory mapping with the Sketch Maps is the key part of using the Sketch Map Tool. The usability of your collected data depends on the local knowledge of the participants and how it is captured by 'sketching' on the maps. The idea of this exercise is to recreate such a mapping session in order to learn more about challenges you could face during the mapping process.
trans-unit.target: Participatory mapping with the Sketch Maps is the key part of using the Sketch Map Tool. The usability of your collected data depends on the local knowledge of the participants and how it is captured by 'sketching' on the maps. The idea of this exercise is to recreate such a mapping session in order to learn more about challenges you could face during the mapping process.

trans-unit.source: The facilitators are doing an EVCA in one area of the prepared case and focus on creating an exposure and hazard map.
trans-unit.target: The facilitators are doing an EVCA in one area of the prepared case and focus on creating an exposure and hazard map.

trans-unit.source: The community members are taking part in this mapping by providing information. However, they also have their own concerns or challenges they wish to address during the mapping process.
trans-unit.target: The community members are taking part in this mapping by providing information. However, they also have their own concerns or challenges they wish to address during the mapping process.

trans-unit.source: 1. Prepare for the roleplay
trans-unit.target: 1. Prepare for the roleplay

trans-unit.source: Separate into groups: For each case study, there should be 2 groups:
trans-unit.target: Separate into groups: For each case study, there should be 2 groups:

trans-unit.source: One group represents the community members (4-7 participants).
trans-unit.target: One group represents the community members (4-7 participants).

trans-unit.source: One group represents the facilitators (2-3 participants). If your total group is too big, you can split and use the same case study twice.
trans-unit.target: One group represents the facilitators (2-3 participants). If your total group is too big, you can split and use the same case study twice.

trans-unit.source: Read the case study carefully.
trans-unit.target: Read the case study carefully.

trans-unit.source: Get into your role.
trans-unit.target: Get into your role.

trans-unit.source: Discuss with the other participants with your role (the community members or the facilitators) and prepare yourself. What do you need to do during the mapping process?
trans-unit.target: Discuss with the other participants with your role (the community members or the facilitators) and prepare yourself. What do you need to do during the mapping process?

trans-unit.source: __Facilitators__
trans-unit.target: __Facilitators__

trans-unit.source: The group of facilitators will separate from the rest in order to prepare their activity.
trans-unit.target: The group of facilitators will separate from the rest in order to prepare their activity.

trans-unit.source: How do I best explain to the community why we do this activity and what is its added value, especially for them? How do I make them feel comfortable to share their information (concerning data protections, ethics)?
trans-unit.target: How do I best explain to the community why we do this activity and what is its added value, especially for them? How do I make them feel comfortable to share their information (concerning data protections, ethics)?

trans-unit.source: What do we want to find out when talking to the community? What do we want to map? What data do we want to collect?
trans-unit.target: What do we want to find out when talking to the community? What do we want to map? What data do we want to collect?

trans-unit.source: What questions would we have to raise in order to get the right answers?
trans-unit.target: What questions would we have to raise in order to get the right answers?

trans-unit.source: How can I help community members to orientate on the map?
trans-unit.target: How can I help community members to orientate on the map?

trans-unit.source: What should be mapped and how should it be mapped? For this questions, take a look at the [help page](https://sketch-map-tool.heigit.org/help)-subsection about Data collection in the field.
trans-unit.target: What should be mapped and how should it be mapped? For this questions, take a look at the [help page](https://sketch-map-tool.heigit.org/help)-subsection about Data collection in the field.

trans-unit.source: Familiarise yourself with the map. What can you detect? Are there any landmarks you can use for orientation?
trans-unit.target: Familiarise yourself with the map. What can you detect? Are there any landmarks you can use for orientation?

trans-unit.source: __Community members__
trans-unit.target: __Community members__

trans-unit.source: Make some notes about the hazards and the exposures you think one could have encountered in the past. Guiding questions could be:
trans-unit.target: Make some notes about the hazards and the exposures you think one could have encountered in the past. Guiding questions could be:

trans-unit.source: Which areas were affected?
trans-unit.target: Which areas were affected?

trans-unit.source: How often is this happening?
trans-unit.target: How often is this happening?

trans-unit.source: How severe was this hazard event? (e.g. how high was the flood level)
trans-unit.target: How severe was this hazard event? (e.g. how high was the flood level)

trans-unit.source: Which consequences could this have for individuals? (e.g. destructions of houses, lost livelyhoods)
trans-unit.target: Which consequences could this have for individuals? (e.g. destructions of houses, lost livelyhoods)

trans-unit.source: Distribute some roles: old persons, students, teachers, or other community members
trans-unit.target: Distribute some roles: old persons, students, teachers, or other community members

trans-unit.source: Beyond that, give your roles some characteristics, you can be creative. Some ideas to start with:
trans-unit.target: Beyond that, give your roles some characteristics, you can be creative. Some ideas to start with:

trans-unit.source: very suspicious person, questions a lot if community should be part of this activity and if there is an added value for the community.
trans-unit.target: very suspicious person, questions a lot if community should be part of this activity and if there is an added value for the community.

trans-unit.source: fearful character, wants to participate but is afraid of sharing information and its consequences.
trans-unit.target: fearful character, wants to participate but is afraid of sharing information and its consequences.

trans-unit.source: old person that has lots of knowledge but cannot orientate on the map.
trans-unit.target: old person that has lots of knowledge but cannot orientate on the map.

trans-unit.source: very motivated person that wants to map more that is asked for/needed.
trans-unit.target: very motivated person that wants to map more that is asked for/needed.

trans-unit.source: Which other characters spring to your mind? Maybe someone who just moved to the place, has a lot of question to other participants,...
trans-unit.target: Which other characters spring to your mind? Maybe someone who just moved to the place, has a lot of question to other participants,...

trans-unit.source: 2. Participatory Mapping Simulation
trans-unit.target: 2. Participatory Mapping Simulation

trans-unit.source: __Now it is time to show your acting ability.__ It might seem a bit strange at first, but try to stay in your character. This can help you a lot to understand challenges and motivations later on.
trans-unit.target: __Now it is time to show your acting ability.__ It might seem a bit strange at first, but try to stay in your character. This can help you a lot to understand challenges and motivations later on.

trans-unit.source: The Participatory Mapping Simulation is led by the facilitators:
trans-unit.target: The Participatory Mapping Simulation is led by the facilitators:

trans-unit.source: Present the project and the activity that is planned (mapping project)
trans-unit.target: Present the project and the activity that is planned (mapping project)

trans-unit.source: Welcoming round: Everyone is introducing themselves shortly (e.g. I'm Sam and I'm running a small farm in the 5th generation).
trans-unit.target: Welcoming round: Everyone is introducing themselves shortly (e.g. I'm Sam and I'm running a small farm in the 5th generation).

trans-unit.source: Start the mapping by showing the map and helping the community members to orientate.
trans-unit.target: Start the mapping by showing the map and helping the community members to orientate.

trans-unit.source: Try to map together.
trans-unit.target: Try to map together.

trans-unit.source: The trainer will end this roleplay when the time is over.
trans-unit.target: The trainer will end this roleplay when the time is over.

trans-unit.source: 3. Optional: Upload your created maps
trans-unit.target: 3. Optional: Upload your created maps

trans-unit.source: :::{dropdown} __Upload your created maps and visualize the results in UMAP__ Take a photo of your sketches or at least one sketch by loading them into [sketch-map-tool.heigit.org](https://sketch-map-tool.heigit.org/). On the website, choose 'Digitize your Sketch maps' on the right. Upload all your sketches in .png or .jpg format. You can mark your sketches and simply drag and drop them into the window.
trans-unit.target: :::{dropdown} __Upload your created maps and visualize the results in UMAP__ Take a photo of your sketches or at least one sketch by loading them into [sketch-map-tool.heigit.org](https://sketch-map-tool.heigit.org/). On the website, choose 'Digitize your Sketch maps' on the right. Upload all your sketches in .png or .jpg format. You can mark your sketches and simply drag and drop them into the window.

trans-unit.source: Now, let's take a look at the results in order to understand the community mapping process. We will use the [uMap website](https://umap.openstreetmap.fr/en/)
trans-unit.target: Now, let's take a look at the results in order to understand the community mapping process. We will use the [uMap website](https://umap.openstreetmap.fr/en/)

trans-unit.source: __Load your data__
trans-unit.target: __Load your data__

trans-unit.source: Open the browser of your choice, navigate to the [uMap website](https://umap.openstreetmap.fr/en/) and click on the large green button: `Create a map`.
trans-unit.target: Open the browser of your choice, navigate to the [uMap website](https://umap.openstreetmap.fr/en/) and click on the large green button: `Create a map`.

trans-unit.source: You can load your data into the map frame by clicking on the upwards-arrow button ![](/fig/umap_upload_data_icon.png) in the toolbar on the right side of your screen. The `Import data` window will open on the right side. Click on `Select file`, navigate to the folder you downloaded from the Sketch Map Tool website and select the vector file (`.geojson`).
trans-unit.target: You can load your data into the map frame by clicking on the upwards-arrow button ![](/fig/umap_upload_data_icon.png) in the toolbar on the right side of your screen. The `Import data` window will open on the right side. Click on `Select file`, navigate to the folder you downloaded from the Sketch Map Tool website and select the vector file (`.geojson`).

trans-unit.source: Click on `Open`. uMap will automatically detect that the uploaded geojson-file is vector data. Click on `Import` to load your data to your map canvas.
trans-unit.target: Click on `Open`. uMap will automatically detect that the uploaded geojson-file is vector data. Click on `Import` to load your data to your map canvas.

trans-unit.source: The detected markings of your sketch map should now appear in the map canvas. Take a look around by zooming in and out, or panning the map.
trans-unit.target: The detected markings of your sketch map should now appear in the map canvas. Take a look around by zooming in and out, or panning the map.

trans-unit.source: Compare detected markings with your physical map. Did the tool detect your markings correctly or are some markings missing?
trans-unit.target: Compare detected markings with your physical map. Did the tool detect your markings correctly or are some markings missing?

trans-unit.source: Which markings got detected better than others? What can you learn from this session in order to make better markings in the future?
trans-unit.target: Which markings got detected better than others? What can you learn from this session in order to make better markings in the future?

trans-unit.source: 4. Final discussion and conclusion
trans-unit.target: 4. Final discussion and conclusion

trans-unit.source: Discuss your results and created maps. Are you satisfied with the map? What can you do with this results? What do you to change?
trans-unit.target: Discuss your results and created maps. Are you satisfied with the map? What can you do with this results? What do you to change?

trans-unit.source: Discuss the challenges you encountered, as well as the potentials of conducting participatory mapping projects you recognized. What did you learn?
trans-unit.target: Discuss the challenges you encountered, as well as the potentials of conducting participatory mapping projects you recognized. What did you learn?

trans-unit.source: Discuss what you would do different in the future (e.g. use only one colour, etc.).
trans-unit.target: Discuss what you would do different in the future (e.g. use only one colour, etc.).

trans-unit.source: Discuss the importance of a well planned mapping process in order to have usable results and data.
trans-unit.target: Discuss the importance of a well planned mapping process in order to have usable results and data.
